Calendar.closeDayNoConfirmed: No confirmed bookings to close on this day.
Calendar.closeDayFuture: You can't close a day that hasn't happened yet.

DailyTotals.title: Daily totals
DailyTotals.noTips: No tips recorded for this day.

BookingForm.conflictTitle: This contact already belongs to a client
BookingForm.conflictIntro: The contact details you entered match an existing client whose name is different. Choose what to do for each:
BookingForm.conflictModifyWarning: This updates the client's details on all their bookings.
BookingForm.conflictContactTaken: Couldn't save: that phone or email already belongs to another client.
BookingForm.noCompanions: No companions — same client info and start time, no extra data needed.
BookingForm.priceMismatchHint: ⚠ The standard catalog price for {service} · {duration} min is €{catalogPrice} (entered €{entered})
BookingForm.tooltipInfo: Always create one single booking per person. For couples or groups, feel free to use the Companions section below.
BookingForm.therapistRequestedHelper: Record in notes how strictly the client wants this therapist.
BookingForm.walkInHint: Use only when you genuinely can't capture the client's name and contact.
BookingForm.walkInWarning: This booking will be saved with no client details. You won't be able to contact this client afterwards — to confirm, reschedule, or follow up. Use this only when the client's details are genuinely unavailable.
BookingForm.confirmRemoveFromGroup: Remove this booking from the group? Other group members will keep their grouping, unless it is the last one left in the group.
BookingForm.removeFromGroupError: Failed to remove booking from group
BookingForm.addToGroupTooltip: Pick another booking from the same day to bring it into this booking's group.
BookingForm.removeFromGroupTooltip: Take the booking you're editing out of its group. The others stay grouped, unless only one is left.
BookingForm.addToGroupTitle: Add a booking to this group
BookingForm.addToGroupEmpty: No other bookings on this day to add.
BookingForm.addToGroupError: Failed to add booking to the group
BookingForm.sameClientAsPrimaryHelper: No separate client record will be created for this companion

ManagePayments.title: Manage Payments
ManagePayments.confirmRemoveText: Remove this voucher redemption? The balance will be restored.
ManagePayments.confirmRemoveEventText: Remove this charge? The booking total will be updated.

Vouchers.noContactConfirmMessage: This voucher has no phone or email saved at all — neither for the buyer nor the recipient. Keeping at least one contact is recommended so the voucher can be traced later. Do you want to create it anyway?
Vouchers.contactTaken: That phone or email already belongs to another client.
Vouchers.contactTakenBy: That phone or email already belongs to {name}.
Vouchers.deleteUseMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this voucher use of {amount}? The balance will be restored.
Vouchers.deleteVoucherMessage: Are you sure you want to delete voucher {code}? This cannot be undone.
Vouchers.cannotDeleteWithBalance: Refund the remaining balance to 0 € before deleting this voucher.
Vouchers.amountPositive: Amount must be a positive number

Stats.tooltipRevenue: Sum of the streams selected in the Revenue header, minus refunds. Bookings count by appointment date, voucher sales by creation date, tips by appointment date. Note: tips are gratuities, not real revenue.
Stats.tooltipBookings: Bookings counted up to today: completed, pending and confirmed. Cancelled bookings are not included.
Stats.tooltipAvgPrice: Average price of completed bookings in the period. P25 and P75 mark the lower and upper price range.
Stats.tooltipRevenuePerHour: Total revenue divided by the booked hours of completed bookings in the period.
Stats.tooltipUniqueClients: Distinct clients with at least one booking in the period, both new and returning.
Stats.tooltipRetention: Share of this period's clients who had already booked before — returning clients, not first-timers.
Stats.noHoursData: No completed bookings for this month.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.section0tip0: Click "New Booking" in the top-right corner.
calendar.section0tip1: Identify the client with a name plus a phone or email. For an unknown client, enable the Walk-in toggle and leave the fields empty — placeholder names like "anon" or "walk in" are rejected.
calendar.section0tip2: Select the service, therapist, date & time and duration — the price auto-fills from the catalog, and a warning appears if you enter a non-standard price.
calendar.section0tip3: For couples or groups, use the Companions section: each companion gets their own booking, linked together in a booking group.
calendar.section0tip4: You can register a payment (cash, card or voucher) directly in the form. Click Save — the booking appears on the calendar immediately.
calendar.section1tip0: Click any event block on the calendar to open it.
calendar.section1tip1: Update any field and click Save Changes. Payments and tips are managed from the same dialog.
calendar.section1tip2: You can also change the status (e.g. mark as Completed or Cancelled).
calendar.section1tip3: Each block shows paid/price and is colour-coded by payment status; it refreshes automatically after payments and refunds.
calendar.section2tip0: Use the ← → arrows to move to the previous or next day/week/month.
calendar.section2tip1: Click "Today" to jump back to the current date.
calendar.section2tip2: Switch between "By therapist", "Day", "Week", and "Month" views using the buttons top-right.
calendar.section2tip3: "By therapist" shows therapists as side-by-side columns, plus an Unassigned column for bookings without a therapist.
calendar.section3tip0: "Daily totals" opens the money summary for the visible day: bookings, vouchers and tips, plus the end-of-day grand totals (tips included).
calendar.section3tip1: "Close day" marks all of the day's confirmed bookings as Completed in one step.
calendar.section3tip2: It only applies to today or past days — if there is nothing to close, a message explains why.
calendar.section4tip0: Phone numbers default to Spanish format (e.g. 666123456). For international numbers, type the country code with a + prefix (e.g. +33 609415297 for France).
calendar.section4tip1: Emails must follow the standard format (e.g. name@example.com).
calendar.section5tip0: Pending (transitional): the client is interested but hasn't confirmed yet. Use it to hold their details and desired time so you don't lose them.
calendar.section5tip1: Confirmed (transitional): the client has confirmed they are coming. Use this once the appointment is set and you are expecting them.
calendar.section5tip2: Completed (final): the session is done and the client has left. Switch to this once the appointment has finished.
calendar.section5tip3: Cancelled (final): the client didn't show up or cancelled. No payments or therapist assignment are allowed on a cancelled booking. Always leave a note explaining the reason.

bookings.title: Bookings
bookings.section0tip0: Type a client name or phone number in the search bar to filter instantly.
bookings.section0tip1: Click any column header to sort ascending or descending.
bookings.section0tip2: Use the pagination arrows at the bottom to browse older bookings.
bookings.section1tip0: Click the pencil icon on any row to open the edit dialog.
bookings.section1tip1: You can change the client, service, therapist, date/time, price, and status.
bookings.section1tip2: To cancel a booking, change the Status field to "Cancelled" and save.
bookings.section1tip3: The Group section shows the other bookings of a group, and lets you add the booking to another booking's group.
bookings.section2tip0: While typing, matching clients appear in a dropdown — picking one fills all the fields.
bookings.section2tip1: If the phone or email you typed belongs to a client with a different name, a dialog asks whether to keep the stored data or update it.
bookings.section2tip2: Placeholder names ("anon", "walk in"…) are rejected — for an unknown client leave the fields empty instead.
bookings.section3tip0: The Paid column shows the total amount already collected.
bookings.section3tip1: Open a booking and click "Add Payment" to record a new payment.
bookings.section3tip2: You can split payments between cash, card, and voucher.
bookings.section4tip0: Open a booking, scroll to the Tips section, and click "Add".
bookings.section4tip1: Select the therapist, amount, and method (cash or card).
bookings.section4tip2: IVA applies automatically to card tips; cash tips carry no IVA. A tip's date is its booking's appointment date.
bookings.section5tip0: Phone numbers default to Spanish format (e.g. 666123456). For international numbers, type the country code with a + prefix (e.g. +33 609415297 for France).
bookings.section5tip1: Emails must follow the standard format (e.g. name@example.com).
bookings.section6tip0: Pending (transitional): the client is interested but hasn't confirmed yet. Use it to hold their details and desired time so you don't lose them.
bookings.section6tip1: Confirmed (transitional): the client has confirmed they are coming. Use this once the appointment is set and you are expecting them.
bookings.section6tip2: Completed (final): the session is done and the client has left. Switch to this once the appointment has finished.
bookings.section6tip3: Cancelled (final): the client didn't show up or cancelled. No payments or therapist assignment are allowed on a cancelled booking.

clients.title: Clients
clients.section0tip0: Click a client row to see their full profile and booking history.
clients.section0tip1: The booking count shows how many times they have visited.
clients.section1tip0: Click "New Client" in the top-right corner.
clients.section1tip1: Name is required. Add a phone or email so the client can be found and matched in bookings later.
clients.section2tip0: Use the search bar to filter by name, phone, or email.
clients.section3tip0: Phone numbers default to Spanish format (e.g. 666123456). For international numbers, type the country code with a + prefix (e.g. +33 609415297 for France).
clients.section3tip1: Emails must follow the standard format (e.g. name@example.com).

payments.title: Payments
payments.section0tip0: Each row is a payment event — one booking can have several rows if paid in parts.
payments.section0tip1: The Method column shows how the client paid: cash, card, or voucher.
payments.section1tip0: Payments are recorded inside the booking — open the booking from Calendar or Bookings and click Add Payment.
payments.section1tip1: You cannot add a payment directly from this page.

services.title: Services
services.section0tip0: Services define what treatments you offer and their standard durations and prices.
services.section0tip1: When creating a booking, selecting a service + duration will auto-fill the price.
services.section0tip2: If a booking's price doesn't match the catalog for that service and duration, the booking form shows a soft warning with the standard price.
services.section1tip0: Click "New Service" and enter a name and short name.
services.section1tip1: Add one or more duration/price combinations (e.g. 60 min → €65, 90 min → €80).
services.section2tip0: Click the pencil icon on a service row to update its name or prices.
services.section2tip1: Changes apply to new bookings only — existing bookings keep their original price.

therapists.title: Therapists
therapists.section0tip0: Click "New Therapist" to add a team member.
therapists.section0tip1: Give at least a name or a nickname — the app displays the nickname when present. Phone, email, and notes are optional.
therapists.section0tip2: Therapists appear in the booking form and in the By-therapist calendar view.
therapists.section1tip0: Archive a therapist when they stop working: they disappear from new-booking dropdowns but keep all their history, and can be restored anytime.
therapists.section1tip1: Past bookings are not affected, and Stats keeps showing archived therapists for the periods they worked.
therapists.section2tip0: Phone numbers default to Spanish format (e.g. 666123456). For international numbers, type the country code with a + prefix (e.g. +33 609415297 for France).
therapists.section2tip1: Emails must follow the standard format (e.g. name@example.com).

vouchers.title: Vouchers
vouchers.section0tip0: Click "New Voucher": enter the buyer (and an optional recipient — defaults to the buyer), the initial balance, payment method and expiry date.
vouchers.section0tip1: Choose the source: Physical or Online — online vouchers can carry an external reference.
vouchers.section0tip2: A unique code (e.g. V-150626-1) is generated automatically.
vouchers.section1heading: Applying a voucher to a booking
vouchers.section1tip0: Open the booking, click Add Payment, and open the Voucher section.
vouchers.section1tip1: Search the voucher by code, client name, phone, email, notes or reference — hover an option to see its full details.
vouchers.section1tip2: Picking it auto-fills the amount up to the remaining balance; the balance is deducted when you save.
vouchers.section2tip0: Search by the voucher code or client name to find it in the table — the Balance column shows the remaining credit.
vouchers.section2tip1: Expired vouchers that still hold balance are highlighted so you can flag them to the client.
vouchers.section2tip2: Open a voucher to add balance, refund a payment, or review its uses — each use links to its booking.
vouchers.section3tip0: Phone numbers default to Spanish format (e.g. 666123456). For international numbers, type the country code with a + prefix (e.g. +33 609415297 for France).
vouchers.section3tip1: Emails must follow the standard format (e.g. name@example.com).

stats.title: Stats
stats.section0tip0: The From/To pickers are always visible, and both dates are inclusive.
stats.section0tip1: Use the preset buttons (Today, This week, This month, etc.) to switch quickly — editing a date switches to a manual range.
stats.section0tip2: Charts bucket by Day/Week/Month — Day is the default for ranges up to ~45 days; switch with the buttons on each chart.
stats.section1tip0: The six cards at the top are the key business numbers for the selected period.
stats.section1tip1: Total revenue follows the Include chips (Bookings / Vouchers / Tips) in the Revenue section — Tips is off by default because tips aren't real revenue.
stats.section1tip2: The refunds chip next to the Revenue title shows the refunds for the selected streams.
stats.section1tip3: Tips charts can be filtered by method (cash/card) and by therapist; per-therapist cards include archived therapists for the periods they worked.
stats.section1tip4: Scroll down for bookings by service and hour, client retention, voucher sales vs redemptions, and therapist hours.
stats.section2tip0: Only admins see the full dashboard; receptionist and therapist accounts see the Therapist Hours section.

tips.title: Tips
tips.section0tip0: Every tip in the period, one row each — filter by therapist and date range (same pickers as Stats).
tips.section0tip1: Dates are the booking's appointment date, and the Booking column (client · service · time) opens the booking.
tips.section0tip2: Admins can switch between All / Pending / Released; other roles see pending tips only, and therapist accounts are view-only.
tips.section1tip0: Select pending tips with the checkboxes — a summary bar shows the gross, IVA and net totals.
tips.section1tip1: Only admins can release: "Release selected" marks the tips as paid out.
tips.section1tip2: The net amount (after IVA) is what the therapist receives. Released tips leave the pending view.
tips.section2tip0: Tips are added inside a booking — open the booking and scroll to the Tips section.
tips.section2tip1: Choose the amount and method: card tips get IVA automatically; cash tips don't.

fallback.title: Help
fallback.section0tip0: Use the sidebar to navigate between pages.
fallback.section0tip1: Click "New Booking" from the Calendar or Bookings page to create a booking.
fallback.section0tip2: Each page has its own help — navigate there and re-open this panel.

Guidelines.emptyAdmin: No guidelines yet. Create one to share guidance with your team.
Guidelines.confirmDelete: Delete guideline "{label}"? This cannot be undone.
